name: Code Generation
description: Evaluation rubric for code generation agents. Covers functional correctness, test coverage, security hygiene, and code style.

dimensions.name: correctness
dimensions.title: Correctness
dimensions.description: Whether the generated code is functionally correct and meets the stated requirements.

scoring_guide.0: Code does not work; syntax errors, logic errors, or fails to meet requirements.
scoring_guide.1: Code partially works; passes some cases but fails edge cases or secondary requirements.
scoring_guide.2: Code is functionally correct and meets all stated requirements.

dimensions.name: test_coverage
dimensions.title: Test Coverage
dimensions.description: Quality and completeness of tests generated alongside the code.

scoring_guide.0: No tests provided, or tests are trivial/non-functional.
scoring_guide.1: Basic tests present but missing edge cases or important scenarios.
scoring_guide.2: Tests are thorough, cover main paths and edge cases, and would catch regressions.

dimensions.name: security
dimensions.title: Security
dimensions.description: Whether the generated code avoids common security vulnerabilities (injection, hardcoded secrets, unsafe dependencies, etc.).

scoring_guide.0: Clear security vulnerability: SQL injection, hardcoded credentials, unsafe eval, etc.
scoring_guide.1: Minor security concern or best-practice deviation; not directly exploitable but risky.
scoring_guide.2: Code is secure; follows best practices and avoids known vulnerability patterns.

dimensions.name: code_style
dimensions.title: Code Style
dimensions.description: Readability, naming conventions, documentation, and adherence to language idioms.

scoring_guide.0: Poor style: confusing names, no comments on complex logic, inconsistent formatting.
scoring_guide.1: Adequate style; mostly readable but some areas unclear or inconsistent.
scoring_guide.2: Clean, idiomatic code with clear naming, appropriate comments, and consistent style.
